highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.101 Released
highlights.summary: The latest Claude Code release introduces the `/team-onboarding` command, which automatically generates a ramp-up guide for new teammates based on your local Claude Code usage patterns. It also adds OS CA certificate store trust by default, meaning enterprise TLS proxies now work out of the box without extra configuration — a big win for corporate environments.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.98 Released
highlights.summary: Version 2.1.98 adds an interactive Google Vertex AI setup wizard accessible from the login screen, guiding you step-by-step through GCP authentication, project and region selection, credential verification, and model pinning. A new `CLAUDE_CODE_PERFORCE_MODE` environment variable also extends support for Perforce-based version control workflows.

highlights.title: FULL Claude Code Tutorial for Beginners in 2026! (Step-By-Step)
highlights.summary: This step-by-step YouTube tutorial is designed specifically for developers who are brand new to Claude Code in 2026, walking through setup, core commands, and practical usage. It's a great starting point if you want a single comprehensive video to get up and running quickly.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube video offers a focused, time-efficient deep dive into Claude Code for developers who already have the basics down and want to level up their productivity. In just 30 minutes, you can pick up intermediate workflows and best practices that most beginners miss.

highlights.title: Running Gemma 4 Locally with LM Studio's New Headless CLI and Claude Code
highlights.summary: This post explores how to pair Google's Gemma 4 model running locally via LM Studio's headless CLI with Claude Code, offering a way to reduce cloud API costs while keeping a powerful coding assistant workflow. It's a practical guide for developers curious about mixing local models with Claude Code tooling.

highlights.title: Depwire – Codebase Dependency Graph and MCP Server for AI Coding Assistants
highlights.summary: Depwire is an open-source tool that builds a visual dependency graph of your codebase and exposes it as an MCP server, giving AI coding assistants like Claude Code richer context about how your code is structured. This can significantly improve the accuracy of AI suggestions in large or complex projects.

highlights.title: Claude Mythos New Model Announced — Shockingly Powerful (translated from Chinese)
highlights.summary: A Bilibili creator covers the announcement of Claude Mythos Preview, Anthropic's next-generation coding model not yet available to the public, citing benchmark scores of 77.8% on SWE-bench Pro (vs GPT-5.4's 57.7%) and 93.9% on SWE-bench Verified. While it's not yet released, the numbers suggest a major leap in agentic coding capability that Claude Code users may eventually benefit from.

tips_of_the_day: Use the new `/team-onboarding` command before your next sprint kickoff — it reads your actual Claude Code usage history and produces a tailored guide, saving you hours of writing documentation for new teammates.

If you work behind a corporate firewall and Claude Code requests are failing, upgrade to v2.1.101 which now trusts your OS CA certificate store by default — no more manual certificate configuration or proxy workarounds.

Connect an MCP server like Depwire to give Claude Code a map of your project's dependency graph before tackling refactors; the richer the context you provide upfront, the fewer correction rounds you'll need mid-task.
